title: ATAH Referral Proposal (Component 3)
description: ATAH Core Protocol object: Component 3 referral-connection proposal record (spec/v0.8 §6.2). A proposing professional asks ATAH to record a pending proposal targeting another professional who has set `looking_for_referral_partners: true`. The proposal persists until accepted (folded into a referral-connection record), declined, withdrawn, or silently lapsed. Per F-4, Component 3 actions are governed by professional delegated authority recorded via principal-delegation.schema.json and the audit-event log; this schema does NOT carry `consent_receipt_id`. Required for any ATAH-conformant implementation.

proposing_principal_delegation.description: Principal-delegation context under which the proposal was created; per F-4, MUST resolve to `authenticated_actor.actor_type: professional` (or `firm_admin` where applicable) with `authority_basis: professional_delegated_token` (or `firm_delegation`). Consumer consent receipts are not accepted on Component 3 actions.

category_scope.description: Profession categories the proposed referral relationship covers.

proposal_message.description: Optional free-text message from the proposing professional to the target professional. Implementations MAY surface this verbatim to the target's AI agent. v0.8.2 imposes no length cap; per-implementation policies apply.

state.description: Proposal state. `pending` is the only non-terminal state. On `accepted` the proposal is folded into a referral-connection record (this schema's record is then deleted). On `declined`, `withdrawn`, or `lapsed` the proposal record is deleted.

expires_at.description: Default `created_at + 6 months`. Configurable per-category via `referral_proposal_expiry_months` in `profession-categories.json`. After this timestamp, ATAH silently transitions the proposal to `lapsed` and deletes the record.

resolved_at.description: Set when the proposal transitions to a terminal state (accepted, declined, withdrawn, lapsed). Present in audit-event metadata even after the proposal record itself is deleted.

decision_explanation.description: Optional decision-explanation object attached to Component 3 proposal events (creation, response, withdrawal, lapse). Per §11A transparency-as-conformance. Where present, `decision_type` is `proposal`. Per F-5, this is the inner object — the field name `decision_explanation` is the wrapper here, not part of the referenced schema.

examples.proposal_message: Frequent cross-referrals for cross-border M&A; would value a recorded ATAH connection.
